title: Theres No I In America (2)

jokes.text: No! I need a 'Previously on'!
jokes.punchline: Demands exposition device from the show itself
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on TV exposition tropes, economical and self-aware

jokes.text: Nothing. Dammit!
jokes.setup: Jack catches Liz trying to leave
jokes.punchline: Liz's frustrated admission of being caught
jokes.explanation: Simple frustrated reaction beat, competent but unremarkable

jokes.text: I need you to marry me so I don't get sent back to Canada!
jokes.punchline: For a fake marriage to avoid deportation
jokes.explanation: Familiar sitcom trope but delivered with urgency and specificity

jokes.text: Really? Now you have a thing?
jokes.setup: Another character needs help at inconvenient time
jokes.punchline: Liz's exasperated observation about timing
jokes.explanation: Relatable frustration, good character voice but not exceptional

jokes.text: Don't know why I'm so worried about bringing my marble collection to work.
jokes.punchline: It's about bringing marbles to work - oddly specific and childish
jokes.explanation: Weird character detail that feels very specific to the character

jokes.text: Butterfingers!
jokes.punchline: Immediately drops them
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate payoff to the setup - classic comedy timing

jokes.text: Why do we have that?
jokes.punchline: Character questions why random object exists
jokes.explanation: Generic observational humor without enough context to land strongly

jokes.text: You're not right for it. What thing? I was born for it!
jokes.setup: Jack dismisses Jenna for unspecified thing
jokes.punchline: Jenna immediately desperate for thing she knows nothing about
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna character moment - ego trumps logic every time

jokes.text: Am I not blonde enough? Because I'll put my head in the microwave!
jokes.setup: Jenna worried about not being right for something
jokes.punchline: Threatens self-harm via kitchen appliance to be more blonde
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly unhinged Jenna logic - vanity meets dangerous stupidity

jokes.text: Jacksonville's naming their new airport, Sized strip club after me. No bottoms!
jokes.punchline: 'Sized' instead of 'side' and anatomical specification
jokes.explanation: Typical Jenna malapropism combined with inappropriate oversharing

jokes.text: The first really, really rich president!
jokes.punchline: Only historic aspect is extreme wealth
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific political satire about Jack's priorities

jokes.text: The one with the guy? It was at night, and I feel like he was standing near a fence...
jokes.setup: Liz trying to defend Obama's accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Can only remember vague visual details
jokes.explanation: Perfect representation of liberal who can't articulate positions

jokes.text: A head has five holes, and they are all needed.
jokes.setup: Liz says country needs Romney like a hole in the head
jokes.punchline: Jack's literal anatomical counter-argument
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literal interpretation of idiom with confident delivery

jokes.text: Because there's no 'I' or 'me' in 'America.' There's both.
jokes.setup: Jenna claims selflessness using letter-spelling cliché
jokes.punchline: Immediately corrected - both letters are there
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of cliché revealing character's narcissism and ignorance

jokes.text: Someone who respects the human fetus and recognizes its value as a hair volumizer.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre beauty-focused view of reproductive politics
jokes.explanation: Shockingly specific character-based take on serious political issue

jokes.text: Like debate? Like DeBarge? I love DeBarge!
jokes.punchline: Jenna mishears as 80s R&B group
jokes.explanation: Good character-appropriate mishearing with pop culture reference

jokes.text: Until this year, I wasn't allowed to vote because Reverend Gary said choosing is a sin.
jokes.punchline: Religious leader banned all choice as sinful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth backstory - absurd religious extremism played straight

jokes.text: Voting is a great American tradition. Like laziness or, eh...
jokes.punchline: Lists laziness as proud tradition, trails off
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy-style casual cynicism about American values

jokes.text: the Parcells have been in this country since we went berserk and murdered everyone at Roanoke.
jokes.punchline: Casually admits ancestral mass murder
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark family history delivered with Kenneth's innocent tone

jokes.text: Black Shrek ran for president?
jokes.setup: Kenneth mentions voting for someone who looks like Tracy
jokes.punchline: Tracy's interpretation of Obama's appearance
jokes.explanation: Unexpected comparison that's both absurd and character-specific

jokes.text: I forgot about that half-nerd.
jokes.punchline: Dismissive description as 'half-nerd'
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy-style casual dismissal with unexpected descriptor

jokes.text: Someone has to be the first person to make a joke after a celebrity dies.
jokes.punchline: Takes pride in inappropriate timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - turning social insensitivity into heroic duty

jokes.text: I guess I was a little 'too soon' with Andy Griffith.
jokes.setup: Tracy admits to inappropriate celebrity death joke timing
jokes.punchline: Specifically about beloved wholesome actor
jokes.explanation: Good specific example of the general principle, Andy Griffith adds wholesome contrast

jokes.text: I can't unhear it! I can't unhear it!
jokes.setup: Tracy reveals what he said about Andy Griffith
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's traumatic reaction to unheard joke
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - audience imagines terrible joke through Kenneth's horror

jokes.text: The first African-American president ever to be up for re-election. Whoo! Historical!
jokes.punchline: Technically accurate but unexciting historical note
jokes.explanation: Good Pete character moment - trying too hard with technically true but lame fact

jokes.text: That was a really good 'American Pie', Pete. You knew all the words. And then you put it on again. And everyone leaves because no one can top it.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining why second time isn't as exciting
jokes.punchline: Compares to karaoke where he kills the mood by repeating success
jokes.explanation: Good extended metaphor that perfectly captures Pete's social awkwardness

jokes.text: You're the one using negative words like 'without' and 'negative'.
jokes.setup: Liz accuses Jack of going negative
jokes.punchline: Jack identifies specific words as inherently negative
jokes.explanation: Clever linguistic argument that's both absurd and technically accurate

jokes.text: The same polyps Jenna had prayed for.
jokes.setup: Attack ad about Liz hoping for Adele's recovery
jokes.punchline: Reveals Jenna prayed for competitor's vocal problems
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Jenna character detail - praying for rival's medical problems

jokes.text: Wrong on cuteness. Wrong on other bitches. Wrong for Jenna.
jokes.punchline: Adapts serious political language for petty personal issues
jokes.explanation: Great parody of political ad language applied to silly personal conflicts

jokes.text: She's a slut monster, and one of gay America's top hags.
jokes.punchline: Uses crude but accurate descriptions of Jenna's appeal
jokes.explanation: Good character insight delivered with inappropriate bluntness

jokes.text: First of all, I have never dropped a hat in my life.
jokes.setup: Liz accuses Jack of betraying people 'at the drop of a hat'
jokes.punchline: Jack takes idiom literally and defends his hat-handling record
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack response - literal interpretation with aristocratic dignity

jokes.text: Herstrionics!
jokes.punchline: Liz creates gendered version of the word
jokes.explanation: Clever feminist wordplay showing Liz's quick wit and politics

jokes.text: Since you've known me, I've been right about no less than everything always.
jokes.punchline: Claims literally perfect accuracy about everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack arrogance - grammatically awkward but supremely confident

jokes.text: Every pair you buy, they give a pair to a child that was forced to work in a factory that makes these shoes. It's not great!
jokes.setup: Liz defending her shoe purchase as charitable
jokes.punchline: Realizes the charity creates circular child labor problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deconstruction of feel-good charity models with dawning realization

jokes.text: You wish, you pervert.
jokes.setup: Liz says maybe Jack should let her 'rub off on him'
jokes.punchline: Jack catches sexual double entendre
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo joke, well-timed but not particularly original

jokes.text: 'Bah-bah-bah-bah-bird bird, bird, bird, b-bird's the word' My gynecologist.
jokes.punchline: Phone rings with embarrassing ringtone during dramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedy timing - undercutting dramatic moment with ridiculous interruption

jokes.text: Did anyone leave a bag of Burger King in your office?
jokes.punchline: Asks about fast food left at medical appointment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - inappropriate priorities and casual grossness

jokes.text: Hey! I'm gonna take the clock apart myself, then put it back together. I wanna see how it works! My friend Jojo did it with a toaster, and it's still good.
jokes.setup: Ballot initiative about clock tower repairs
jokes.punchline: Mayor's reasoning is childlike curiosity and anecdotal evidence
jokes.explanation: Great characterization of small-town politician with childlike reasoning

jokes.text: Ain't nobody touch my clock! It's my wife, and this is where I do my sniperin'!
jokes.punchline: Hermit claims romantic relationship with clock and uses it for sniping
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly unhinged character with multiple surprising revelations

jokes.text: I cast the deciding vote that made 'white' the newest flavor of Mountain Dew. But I guess that's just life in a Dewmocracy.
jokes.punchline: Corporate flavor vote with democracy pun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth line - innocent participation in absurd corporate democracy

jokes.text: Welcome to the first and only debate of the Jenna-ral election.
jokes.punchline: Creates pun with her name and 'general election'
jokes.explanation: Typical Jenna narcissistic wordplay - makes everything about herself

jokes.text: Insincere!
jokes.setup: Someone uses word 'sincerely' in formal context
jokes.punchline: Jenna immediately contradicts with opposite
jokes.explanation: Simple contradiction joke - economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: a woman 'People' magazine once called 'an unnamed friend of the deceased'.
jokes.setup: Jenna describing herself in grandiose terms
jokes.punchline: Quote reveals she was anonymous background source about dead person
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna delusion - treating anonymous mention as prestigious recognition

jokes.text: Ooh, and how do they tell me their decisions? Do I get shocked whenever I do the wrong thing?
jokes.setup: Liz explaining reproductive rights about men making decisions for women
jokes.punchline: Jenna imagines literal shock-based control system
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jenna misunderstanding - literal interpretation of political metaphor

jokes.text: And Jenna Maroney would get every part. That's my America. That's Mitt Romney's America.
jokes.punchline: Realizes she's accidentally making case FOR the cuts from Jenna's perspective
jokes.explanation: Excellent moment where Liz's argument backfires by appealing to Jenna's selfishness

jokes.text: Scarlett Johansson. Blake Lively. Jessica Biel -- Wow. Those are all very beautiful women. Do you really want to be photographed next to them?
jokes.punchline: Jack points out this makes Jenna look worse by comparison
jokes.explanation: Smart political strategy that exploits Jenna's vanity and insecurity

jokes.text: Craig T. Nelson, Chuck Norris, and Charlton Heston's skull. You'd be the only cool Republican.
jokes.punchline: List gets progressively less appealing, ending with dead person's remains
jokes.explanation: Great escalating list that acknowledges Republican celebrity deficit while appealing to Jenna's ego

jokes.text: I stopped you when you tried to drive us into Lake Michigan because Scottie Pippen got married.
jokes.setup: Liz defending her good judgment about Jenna
jokes.punchline: Example reveals Jenna's suicidal reaction to basketball player's wedding
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Jenna backstory - dramatic overreaction to random celebrity news

jokes.text: Miss Lemon, I know Scottie Pippen. I own a Fuddrucker's with Scottie Pippen. And you, sir, look like Scottie Pippen.
jokes.punchline: Parodies famous political debate line with restaurant ownership and appearance
jokes.explanation: Excellent parody of Lloyd Bentsen debate line with absurd restaurant detail

jokes.text: When our founding fathers first set out, time and time again, our nation horizon... prosperity... dreams... freedom. But the spirit... journey... destiny. Mitt Romney values. Jenna values. I've met people. For this generation and generations to come.
jokes.punchline: Completely incoherent word salad of political buzzwords
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of meaningless political rhetoric - pure emotional manipulation

jokes.text: Good God, those are load-bearing balloons! Everyone, run for your lives!
jokes.punchline: Someone realizes balloons are structurally important to building
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist construction joke - balloons as architectural elements

jokes.text: Columbus thought he was in India! And did he worry about being wrong? No! He just called everybody Indians. And we still do it today! Why? 'Cause.
jokes.punchline: Uses Columbus mistake as positive example with casual 'cause' ending
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - historical ignorance as American tradition with casual dismissal

jokes.text: Just like when we named this country after the fourth or fifth guy who discovered it. Amerigo Vespucci? Who cares? America!
jokes.setup: Tracy continuing theme about American ignorance
jokes.punchline: Dismisses actual historical figure behind country's name
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to Columbus joke with same theme of proud ignorance

jokes.text: I take thousands of micronaps during the day.
jokes.setup: Liz asks how Jack sleeps at night
jokes.punchline: Jack's bizarre sleep schedule involving tiny naps
jokes.explanation: Great Jack character detail - hyperefficient approach to basic human need

jokes.text: partly because when I have it down, it looks like this...
jokes.setup: Liz explaining why she keeps her head high
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates unflattering chin situation
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating physical comedy moment for Liz

jokes.text: The man doesn't drink. How does he let a moment land?
jokes.punchline: Alcohol as essential for comedic timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack logic - judging politicians by cocktail party performance standards

jokes.text: Latah, Mah! I'm goin' down the pahk to play ball! I sure love Americer! Almost as much as I love chowdah --
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated Boston accent with stereotypical activities
jokes.explanation: Good accent comedy showing Jack's working-class origins

jokes.text: Oh, my Gawd, it's fewtchah me!
jokes.setup: Young Jack seeing his future self
jokes.punchline: Boston accent pronunciation of 'future me'
jokes.explanation: Continuation of accent joke with time travel element

jokes.text: It certainly won't work with a man who didn't cry at 'Field of Dreams', not even when the bank failed to recoup its investment in the farm.
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was emotional about financial aspects, not sentimental ones
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack character moment - subverts expectation about what would move him emotionally

jokes.text: But you can't get a decent iced tea there. Forget it!
jokes.setup: Liz threatens to move to France
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandons plan due to beverage concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - dramatic gesture undermined by mundane priorities

jokes.text: Remember that time you got a haircut, and I was the first person to say, 'Someone got a haircut'? You owe me!
jokes.setup: Pete trying to call in favor
jokes.punchline: Favor is stating obvious about someone's appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete desperation - treating basic social observation as major favor

jokes.text: Look, get a hobby, like me and my gymnastics!
jokes.punchline: Suggests gymnastics as hobby for adult man
jokes.explanation: Good unexpected character detail - adult doing children's sport

jokes.text: A Facebook that wasn't crawling with old bald guys!
jokes.punchline: Specific complaint about Facebook demographics
jokes.explanation: Relatable social media observation with Pete's self-deprecating awareness

jokes.text: why don't you kiss a potato like the rest of us?
jokes.setup: Someone mocking Pete's need for romantic contact
jokes.punchline: Suggests potato-kissing as normal alternative
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd suggestion that implies widespread potato romance

jokes.text: I was gonna drive a sports car and say cool stuff like, 'Wiki-what?'!
jokes.punchline: His idea of 'cool' is confused Wikipedia reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete character detail - his profoundly uncool idea of coolness

jokes.text: Screw you, Dona-one-d Estaluj. I don't need Viagra! You need Viagra!
jokes.punchline: Fighting with obvious spambot using childish insults
jokes.explanation: Great Jenna moment - taking obvious spam personally and fighting back

jokes.text: It stands for Motorized Intelligent Technodrome Termina--
jokes.punchline: Creates absurd robotic acronym
jokes.explanation: Good absurd explanation that makes Romney sound like a robot

jokes.text: I want to send Estaluj a picture, but it's too dark inside my pants.
jokes.punchline: Reveals inappropriate photo location
jokes.explanation: Typical Jenna inappropriate oversharing in casual context

jokes.text: It's just kids' bike shorts for fatties!
jokes.punchline: Jenna's crude dismissal of the business model
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna cynicism - reducing successful business to crude basics

jokes.text: when I was a boy, I used to go to Fenway and sell my urine... for fans to throw at Mickey Mantle.
jokes.punchline: Sold bodily fluids for baseball fan assault
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disturbing Jack backstory - entrepreneurial spirit in horrifying context

jokes.text: Voters don't really pick the president. That's up to important people like corporations and celebrities.
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals how elections actually work
jokes.explanation: Dark but accurate political commentary delivered with Jack's casual elitism

jokes.text: if I stop saving for business school and started saving for implants, I could be just like her.
jokes.punchline: Chooses cosmetic surgery over education
jokes.explanation: Dark commentary on celebrity influence and young women's priorities

jokes.text: I wasn't watching cute little kitten videos! I was watching pornography! Who put these sleepy kitten videos on here?
jokes.punchline: Preemptively defends against wrong embarrassing thing
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy logic - being caught with wholesome content is more embarrassing

jokes.text: Less if I count genocides as celebrities.
jokes.setup: Tracy estimating how quickly he'd get banned again
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions making jokes about mass murder
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark Tracy line delivered with casual matter-of-factness

jokes.text: No, I can say that word. I'm black. But Dick Clark wasn't!
jokes.setup: Tracy about to make inappropriate celebrity death joke
jokes.punchline: Defends using slur based on his race, notes deceased wasn't same race
jokes.explanation: Typical Tracy inappropriate logic about racial language boundaries

jokes.text: Hey, sugarlips. Let's make it like a black magazine and jet.
jokes.setup: Cool version of Pete hitting on Maria
jokes.punchline: Combines racial reference with 'let's jet' expression
jokes.explanation: Shows contrast between normal and cool Pete with questionable wordplay

jokes.text: My 2012 Corvette's parked outside. 'Cause I always find a parking spot.
jokes.punchline: Brags about mundane parking success
jokes.explanation: Great character detail - even cool Pete's bragging is about practical matters

jokes.text: Well, well, well, well, well, well, well, well... Nope, it's too many wells. I'm gonna re-enter.
jokes.punchline: Recognizes she overdid the dramatic wells and starts over
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-moment where character critiques her own dramatic timing

jokes.text: Just like most games.
jokes.setup: Liz says 'two can play at that game'
jokes.punchline: Jack's literal observation about game participation
jokes.explanation: Good Jack literal interpretation with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: You cynical, manipulative, cold-blooded Adonis.
jokes.punchline: Insult becomes compliment with 'Adonis'
jokes.explanation: Good escalating insult that reveals Liz's attraction despite anger

jokes.text: Yours dripped on me, and now I'm drunk!
jokes.setup: Discussion about rubbing off on each other
jokes.punchline: Makes statement sound sexual and intoxicating
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo joke with moderate character integration

jokes.text: All day, all night, baby.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking if Pete feels hopeful
jokes.punchline: Cool Pete's catchphrase response to sincere question
jokes.explanation: Good ending callback showing Pete's maintained transformation

tmdb_overview: Realizing that Jenna could decide the Presidential election, Liz and Jack stage a debate to win her support and her followers. Kenneth turns to Tracy for advice on how to be an informed voter, while Pete tries to recreate the magic of the 2008 election.
insight: Two-part finale hits 82 jokes across network satire and character breakdown.
